tweets.title: "Capability in developing long range surface-to-air missile system is possessed only by a handful of nations", India's defence minister Rajnath Singh says after testing long range Kusha surface to air missile
tweets.content:
"Capability in developing long range surface-to-air missile system is possessed only by a handful of nations", India's defence minister Rajnath Singh says after testing long range Kusha surface to air missile

Rajnath Singh (@rajnathsingh)

The @DRDO_India has successfully conducted the maiden flight test of Long-Range Surface-to-Air Missile Kusha today from the APJ Abdul Kalam Island off the coast of Odisha.

Kusha missile system is capable of neutralising wide variety of aerial threats including fighter jets, missiles, UAVs and large enemy aircrafts within a wide range and altitude envelope.

Today’s successful test flight of Kusha marks an important milestone in Indian defence R&D. The capability in developing long range surface-to-air missile system is possessed only by a handful of nations. This will also eliminate the import dependency for such systems and signifies a giant leap in air defence capability of the nation. I congratulate DRDO and other stakeholders for this achievement.

tweets.title: BREAKING: Delhi Police Commissioner Granted NSA Detention Powers for 3 Months The Centre has authorised the Delhi Police Commissioner to exercise powers under the National Security Act (NSA), from 19 July to 18 October 2026, as per a Delhi Gazette notification. The NSA allows preventive detention of individuals for up to 12 months if authorities believe it is necessary to prevent acts prejudicial to national security, public order, or essential services. Such detention can be ordered without immediate formal charges or a criminal trial, subject to safeguards and periodic review under the Act.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: Delhi Police Commissioner Granted NSA Detention Powers for 3 Months

The Centre has authorised the Delhi Police Commissioner to exercise powers under the National Security Act (NSA), from 19 July to 18 October 2026, as per a Delhi Gazette notification.

The NSA allows preventive detention of individuals for up to 12 months if authorities believe it is necessary to prevent acts prejudicial to national security, public order, or essential services. Such detention can be ordered without immediate formal charges or a criminal trial, subject to safeguards and periodic review under the Act.

tweets.title: India Targets 5 Indigenous Small Modular Reactors by 2033 The Government has announced plans to make at least five indigenous Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) operational by 2033, as part of its long-term nuclear energy strategy. BARC is developing a 220 MW Bharat SMR, a 55 MW SMR, and a high-temperature gas-cooled reactor capable of producing hydrogen. India aims to expand its nuclear power capacity from 8.78 GW today to ~22 GW by 2031–32, with a long-term target of 100 GW by 2047 under the Nuclear Energy Mission.

● Jordan's military intercepted five Iranian drones and three ballistic missiles targeting the kingdom. Bahrain's military similarly reported shooting down multiple Iranian aerial aggressions.

● The Lebanese Armed Forces claimed the Israeli military opened fire near its troops as Lebanese soldiers began deploying into the Zawtar al-Gharbiyeh pilot zone in southern Lebanon. EAM highlighted several concrete steps taken toward normalization over the past few months, including: ▪️ Resumption of direct flights between India and China ▪️ Restarting the Kailash Mansarovar Yatra ▪️ Recommencing bilateral border trade ▪️ Updating the visa regime Despite the recent border de-escalation, Jaishankar clearly outlined the remaining structural issues that India expects to be addressed: ▪️ Ensuring fair market access for Indian goods ▪️ Correcting the ongoing trade imbalance ▪️ Securing predictability in supply chains
tweets.content:
EAM Dr. S. Jaishankar met with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi to review the gradual normalization of India-China relations since the October 2024 Kazan summit.

India’s stance remains firm: peace and tranquility in the border areas is the absolute prerequisite for normal ties.

EAM highlighted several concrete steps taken toward normalization over the past few months, including:
▪️ Resumption of direct flights between India and China
▪️ Restarting the Kailash Mansarovar Yatra
▪️ Recommencing bilateral border trade
▪️ Updating the visa regime

Despite the recent border de-escalation, Jaishankar clearly outlined the remaining structural issues that India expects to be addressed:
▪️ Ensuring fair market access for Indian goods
▪️ Correcting the ongoing trade imbalance
▪️ Securing predictability in supply chains

tweets.content:
BREAKING: Terrorists Attack J&K Police Party in Anantnag

Terrorists opened fire on a Jammu & Kashmir Police party at Lal Chowk, Anantnag, along the Kokernag–Anantnag Road, around 12:30 PM on July 22.

Head Constable Ashiq Hussain of the IRP 3rd Battalion, who was deployed for Amarnath Yatra security, sustained critical injuries and later succumbed at GMC Anantnag.

Police personnel returned fire, but the terrorists managed to flee the scene. Additional security forces have been rushed to the area, and a massive search operation is underway to trace the attackers.

tweets.title: Government Introduces 50% CAPF Quota for Ex-Agniveers The Government has informed Parliament that 50% of Constable (GD) and Rifleman vacancies in the CRPF, BSF, CISF, ITBP, SSB, and Assam Rifles will be reserved for Ex-Agniveers. Key provisions: • No written examination • Exemption from Physical Standards Test (PST) and Physical Efficiency Test (PET) • 3-year age relaxation for all Ex-Agniveers • 5-year one-time age relaxation for the first Agniveer batch • A dedicated Ex-Agniveer Wing has been created in the Ministry of Home Affairs. The first batch of Agniveers has not yet completed its four-year tenure, so the policy is yet to be implemented through its first recruitment cycle.
tweets.content:
Government Introduces 50% CAPF Quota for Ex-Agniveers

The Government has informed Parliament that 50% of Constable (GD) and Rifleman vacancies in the CRPF, BSF, CISF, ITBP, SSB, and Assam Rifles will be reserved for Ex-Agniveers.

Key provisions:
• No written examination
• Exemption from Physical Standards Test (PST) and Physical Efficiency Test (PET)
• 3-year age relaxation for all Ex-Agniveers
• 5-year one-time age relaxation for the first Agniveer batch
• A dedicated Ex-Agniveer Wing has been created in the Ministry of Home Affairs.

The first batch of Agniveers has not yet completed its four-year tenure, so the policy is yet to be implemented through its first recruitment cycle.

tweets.title: RT by @NetramDefence: A two-day brainstorming workshop on Naval Stealth-Beyond 2030 was organized by NSTL, Visakhapatnam, during 20–21 July 2026, bringing together key stakeholders from DRDO, Indian Navy, Defence Shipyards and Industry to deliberate on future directions in naval stealth technologies. The workshop was inaugurated by Dr. R V Hara Prasad, DG(NS&M) on 20th July in the presence of R Adm Sujit Baxi, DG WDB, and Director, NSTL. During the workshop, NSTL handed over Technology for Fabrication, Installation & Commissioning of the LM2500 Gas Turbine Infrared Suppression System for Naval Vessel to BHEL Over 125 delegates representing DRDO Laboratories, Indian Navy, Defence Shipyards, and Industry attended the workshop. Technical sessions covered a broad spectrum of topics, including Radar Cross Section, Infrared Signature Management, Acoustic Stealth, ELFE and Magnetic Signatures, Integrated Multi-Spectral Stealth Concepts, and Future Naval Stealth Technologies Beyond 2030.

In a major breakthrough against cross-border arms smuggling, Bikaner Police have arrested three more accused linked to a Pakistan-based drone weapons trafficking network. Investigators revealed that the accused were in direct contact with the main handler and were responsible for retrieving drone-dropped consignments of illegal weapons using precise GPS coordinates before delivering them to designated recipients. The investigation has exposed an organised cross-border syndicate, and authorities are now expanding the probe to identify additional operatives and links across the India-Pakistan border.
